mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recessionary conditions, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand and residential investment, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistically significant logit coefficient (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production during an economic expansion reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for 5-day forward returns during expansion regimes remains below 0.01, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 210 reflects entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during high CPI periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the threshold effect vanishes when real yields remain positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenue expectations, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for payroll growth remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll changes (Spearman rho < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real consumption expenditures signals deteriorating household balance sheets and unsustainable demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day return direction, or the divergence metric shows zero predictive power out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 110.0 while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0 percent indicates a supply-side contraction that monetary policy fails to offset, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during the specified threshold regime are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the joint condition fails to improve classification accuracy over a baseline model by > 5 percentage points.
